audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:Ik wil ...]], a simple way to say what you want.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces finite modal [[grammar:wil]] in [[grammar:Ik wil N]], with nominal and infinitival complements. The chapter keeps desire expressions bounded and does not introduce the wider modal paradigm.

audienceSections.normal:
#### Ik wil N

Use [[grammar:Ik wil N]] to say what you want. You can also put a known action after [[grammar:wil]], as in [[grammar:Ik wil eten.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### Ik wil N

Current chapter scope: [[grammar:wil]] is an irregular present form of modal [[grammar:willen]]. It selects nominal complements in [[grammar:Ik wil de rijst.]] and bare infinitival complements in [[grammar:Ik wil eten.]] The reading maintains one first-person perspective and does not introduce a wider agreement paradigm.

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Ik wil ...]] is direct. Use it here to understand simple statements about wants.
audienceSections.expert: Bare [[grammar:willen]] expresses volition directly. Pragmatic mitigation and polite request constructions are outside current Normal scope.

audienceSections.normal: These examples use clear everyday Dutch.
audienceSections.expert: The forms are standard; request politeness depends on discourse context and is analysed in later expert material.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Ik wil eten.]] says that Marieke wants to eat.
- [[grammar:Ik wil drinken.]] says that she wants to drink.
- [[grammar:Ik wil de rijst.]] says that she wants the rice.
- [[grammar:Ik wil de soep.]] says that she wants the soup.
- [[grammar:Ik wil kaas.]] uses the same pattern with cheese.
- [[grammar:Ik wil fruit.]] uses the same pattern with fruit.
- [[grammar:Er is een winkel.]] says that a shop is present.
- [[grammar:Ik wil koffie.]] says that she wants coffee.
- [[grammar:Ik wil een banaan.]] names one kind of fruit.
- [[grammar:Ik wil een peer.]] names another kind of fruit.
breakdown.expert:
- [[grammar:Ik wil eten.]] pairs finite modal [[grammar:wil]] with the bare infinitive [[grammar:eten]].
- [[grammar:Ik wil drinken.]] pairs finite modal [[grammar:wil]] with the bare infinitive [[grammar:drinken]].
- [[grammar:Ik wil de rijst.]] uses a definite nominal complement.
- [[grammar:Ik wil de soep.]] uses another definite nominal complement.
- [[grammar:Ik wil kaas.]] uses an articleless mass-noun complement.
- [[grammar:Ik wil fruit.]] uses another articleless mass-noun complement.
- [[grammar:Er is een winkel.]] reuses the Chapter 5 singular existential frame.
- [[grammar:Ik wil koffie.]] supplies a first-person modal clause with an articleless mass noun.
- [[grammar:Ik wil een banaan.]] uses an indefinite count-noun complement and begins the fruit-field expansion.
- [[grammar:Ik wil een peer.]] uses another indefinite count-noun complement in the fruit field.
